sets.passage: Modern cities are increasingly judged not by the height of their towers but by the quality of the public spaces that bind their inhabitants together. For decades, planners (1)____ the motor car above all else, carving wide roads through neighbourhoods and leaving little room for ordinary people to gather. The social cost of that single choice has proved (2)____ , for streets designed solely for traffic rarely invite the casual encounters on which urban community quietly depends. A growing body of research now (3)____ that well-designed squares, parks and pavements strengthen the fragile bonds between strangers. When residents have somewhere pleasant to linger, mutual trust accumulates without anyone (4)____ it. Yet such spaces remain unevenly (5)____ : wealthy districts enjoy generous gardens, while poorer areas are left with cramped lots and a (6)____ handful of benches. Critics argue that this imbalance faithfully (7)____ deeper social inequalities, since access to shade, greenery and safe walking is no trivial luxury. Reformers therefore (8)____ that authorities treat public space as essential infrastructure rather than decorative afterthought. Reclaiming a single street from parked cars may seem a (9)____ gesture, but the cumulative effect of many such interventions is striking. Where citizens are given room to meet, the abstract notion of belonging becomes tangible and (10)____ . The lesson emerging from the most liveable cities is simple: build patiently for people first, and the traffic, the commerce and even the prosperity tend to follow. This implies neither abolishing cars nor romanticising the past; it means restoring a balance that single-minded engineering quietly destroyed. A child walking to a playground, a neighbour resting on a bench, a stranger pausing at a stall, such encounters are the threads from which belonging is woven.
sets.explain_zh: (1) prioritised 优先考虑，过去式及物动词（主语 planners，宾语 the motor car）；(2) considerable 相当大的，作表语形容词修饰 cost，has proved considerable；(3) demonstrates 证明，三单及物动词后接 that 从句（主语 a body of research）；(4) noticing 注意到，介词 without 后接动名词，without anyone noticing it；(5) distributed 分布，be unevenly distributed 被动（空间分布不均）；(6) modest 少量的，a modest handful of benches，形容词修饰 handful（数量不大的几条长椅）；(7) mirrors 反映，三单及物动词（主语 imbalance，宾语 inequalities），与 faithfully 副词搭配；(8) insist 坚持要求，insist that... (should) treat 引导虚拟语气从句的不及物动词；(9) symbolic 象征性的，a symbolic gesture 形容词修饰 gesture（象征性举措，与下文 cumulative effect 的“实质效果”形成对照）；(10) lasting 持久的，作表语形容词与 tangible 并列修饰 belonging。干扰项：ignored(忽视的/过去式)与(1)句 planners 抬高汽车地位的语义相反，且对(5)需中性的 distributed、对(2)(10)的表语义不合，对所有空失败；asleep(睡着的)为表语形容词，仅用于 fall/be asleep，既不作定语(6)(9)，也不能修饰(5)unevenly 后的“分布”义，更非动词，对所有空均不可填；reflection(名词:反映/倒影)非动词，(7)需三单动词 mirrors，且其余各空无名词主语空可填，对所有空失败；metallic(金属的)指材质，作定语既不能修饰(5)unevenly 后的“分布”义，也不与(6)handful、(9)gesture、(10)belonging 构成任何合理搭配，更非动词，对所有空均不可填；rapidly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Scientific scepticism is often misunderstood as a sour refusal to believe anything at all. In truth, it is a disciplined willingness to (1)____ even one's most cherished assumptions to rigorous testing. A genuine sceptic does not reject a claim out of stubborn prejudice; rather, she withholds (2)____ until the evidence has been carefully weighed. This habit of mind is precisely what makes modern science so remarkably (3)____ , for every conclusion remains permanently open to revision. Critics sometimes complain that such caution breeds endless doubt, yet the (4)____ is closer to the truth. By demanding that hypotheses be (5)____ against reality, scepticism steadily filters out the comfortable errors that wishful thinking would otherwise (6)____ . The danger, of course, lies in mistaking mere cynicism for genuine inquiry. A cynic dismisses inconvenient findings without examination, whereas a true sceptic remains (7)____ to persuasion by better data. Distinguishing the two requires a certain intellectual (8)____ that schools rarely teach explicitly. In an age awash with confident assertions and viral misinformation, the ability to ask politely how a speaker (9)____ at a given conclusion has become an indispensable civic skill. Those who cultivate it are far less easily (10)____ by charlatans and propaganda alike. Scepticism, properly understood, is therefore not the enemy of knowledge but its most loyal guardian, forever insisting that belief be earned rather than simply assumed. What distinguishes the genuine inquirer is the readiness to be proved wrong, to surrender a comfortable conviction the moment a more accurate one presents itself. That readiness is far rarer than the casual public imagines, and it is precisely what allows knowledge to advance from one generation to the next.
sets.explain_zh: (1) subject 使经受，subject sth to testing 固定搭配及物动词原形（willingness to subject...）；(2) judgement 判断，withhold judgement 固定搭配名词（暂不下判断）；(3) reliable 可靠的，作表语形容词修饰 science；(4) reverse 相反情况，the reverse is closer to the truth 名词（“事实恰恰相反”，the reverse is true/closer to the truth 固定句式）；(5) tested 检验，be tested against reality 被动（demanding that hypotheses be tested）；(6) preserve 保留，would otherwise preserve 及物动词原形（保留错误）；(7) open 乐于接受的，remain open to persuasion 固定搭配表语形容词；(8) humility 谦逊，intellectual humility 名词（求知的谦逊）；(9) arrived 得出，arrive at a conclusion 固定搭配（后有 at，过去式表已发生），how a speaker arrived at...；(10) deceived 被欺骗，be deceived by 被动。干扰项：verdict(裁决)虽近 judgement，但固定搭配为 withhold judgement，且(4)“the ___ is closer to the truth”表“事实恰相反”需 the reverse，the verdict is closer to the truth 不地道，verdict 对各空失败；expose(暴露)与(6)句 wishful thinking 会“保留/纵容”错误的语义相反，且 filters out 已表“过滤掉”，需 preserve 表对照；honest(诚实的)语义与 intellectual humility 所需(8)的“谦逊”不符，且(7)固定搭配为 open to persuasion 而非 honest to，对各空失败；reached(达到)虽近 arrived，但 reach a conclusion 不接 at，而(9)句末为 at a conclusion，故需 arrived（arrive at），reached 因介词搭配失败；blindly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: A free press has long been celebrated as the indispensable watchdog of any healthy democracy. By holding the powerful to account, journalists help citizens make the informed choices on which self-government ultimately (1)____ . Yet the relationship between media and democracy has grown increasingly (2)____ in the digital era. The collapse of advertising revenue has (3)____ countless local newspapers to close, leaving vast communities with no one to scrutinise their councils or courts. Into that vacuum rush partisan outlets whose chief aim is not to inform but to (4)____ outrage among an already divided public. Algorithms designed to maximise engagement reward the most (5)____ content, and measured reporting struggles to compete with sensational headlines. Citizens, bombarded from every side, find it ever harder to (6)____ reliable fact from confident fabrication. Some observers fear that this confusion is gradually (7)____ the shared reality on which democratic debate depends. Others remain cautiously (8)____ , noting that the same technologies can also amplify suppressed voices and expose hidden corruption. What seems beyond (9)____ is that a citizenry unable to agree on basic facts cannot deliberate sensibly about anything else. Restoring trust will require not only better journalism but a public patient enough to (10)____ inconvenient truths over flattering illusions. None of this can be achieved overnight, for habits of attention, once lost, are slow to relearn. Schools may have to teach the careful reading of evidence as deliberately as they once taught grammar. Democracy, in the end, can be no wiser than the information that sustains it, and no more honest than the citizens who choose what they are willing to believe.
sets.explain_zh: (1) rests 依赖，rest on 固定搭配（后有 on，三单加 s），on which self-government rests；(2) fraught 充满（紧张）的，grown fraught 表语形容词（关系变得紧张复杂）；(3) forced 迫使，have forced sth to close 及物动词过去分词（现在完成 has forced）；(4) provoke 激起，provoke outrage 及物动词原形（激起愤怒）；(5) inflammatory 煽动性的，most inflammatory content 形容词最高级修饰 content；(6) distinguish 区分，distinguish A from B 固定搭配（后有 from）；(7) eroding 侵蚀，is gradually eroding 及物动词现在分词（侵蚀共同现实）；(8) optimistic 乐观的，remain cautiously optimistic 表语形容词；(9) dispute 争议，beyond dispute 固定搭配名词（无可争辩）；(10) favour 偏好，favour A over B 固定搭配及物动词（后有 over）。干扰项：molecular(分子的)为形容词指材质/科学属性，既不能修饰(2)(5)(8)的语境（grown molecular / most molecular content / cautiously molecular 均不通），也非动词，对所有空均不可填；ceramic(陶瓷的)同为材质形容词，与(2)(5)(8)的“关系紧张/内容煽动/态度乐观”语境完全无关，亦非动词，对所有空均不可填；soothe(抚慰)与(4)句煽动愤怒的语义相反；balanced(平衡的)与(5)句 algorithm 奖励“最煽动”内容的语义相反；doubtful(可疑的/怀疑的)与(8)句 cautiously 后应为 optimistic 的语义场不合，且 beyond doubt 虽成立但(9)处需名词 dispute 搭配 beyond，doubtful 为形容词不可填(9)，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: For two centuries, economists treated perpetual growth as both natural and desirable, the reliable cure for almost every social ill. That comfortable assumption is now coming under serious (1)____ from a new generation of thinkers. They point out that the planet's resources are emphatically (2)____ , and that an economy expanding without limit must eventually collide with physical reality. The pursuit of ever-rising output, they argue, has (3)____ environmental degradation on a scale that threatens the very prosperity it was meant to secure. Defenders of growth reply that human ingenuity will always (4)____ a way around scarcity, just as it has so often before. Yet critics counter that some boundaries are simply not (5)____ : a stable climate, fertile soil and breathable air cannot be manufactured to order. The debate has given rise to proposals for a more (6)____ economy, one that measures success by wellbeing rather than by sheer volume of production. Such ideas were once dismissed as hopelessly (7)____ , the fantasies of dreamers indifferent to jobs and wages. Today they receive a markedly more serious (8)____ , even within mainstream institutions. The challenge, of course, is to (9)____ genuine human flourishing from the relentless accumulation of stuff, a distinction that decades of advertising have deliberately blurred. Whether societies can (10)____ to this profound shift in thinking may well determine the habitability of the century to come. For most of the modern era, the verb to grow has carried an unquestioned moral weight, as though more output were always good in itself. Loosening that grip on the public imagination is perhaps the hardest task of all. Yet history offers encouragement, for societies have reorganised their priorities before, and may yet prize sufficiency over excess.
sets.explain_zh: (1) scrutiny 审视，come under scrutiny 固定搭配名词（受到审视）；(2) finite 有限的，resources are finite 表语形容词；(3) accelerated 加速了，has accelerated degradation 及物动词过去分词（现在完成）；(4) find 找到，find a way around 固定搭配及物动词原形（找到绕过办法），will always find；(5) negotiable 可商量/可让步的，are not negotiable 表语形容词（某些边界不可妥协）；(6) sustainable 可持续的，a more sustainable economy 形容词修饰 economy；(7) naive 天真的，dismissed as naive 表语形容词（被斥为天真）；(8) hearing 倾听/审议，receive a serious hearing 固定搭配名词（得到认真对待）；(9) disentangle 区分剥离，disentangle A from B 及物动词原形（后有 from，把繁荣从堆积物中剥离）；(10) adapt 适应，adapt to this shift 不及物动词（后有 to）。干扰项：doubt(怀疑)虽与(1)语义场近，但 come under doubt 不地道，固定为 come under scrutiny，且 doubt 不与其他空搭配；endless(无尽的)与(2)句 resources are ___ 应为“有限”finite 的语义相反；oceanic(海洋的)为材质/属性形容词，与(2)(5)(6)(7)的语境（资源有限/边界不可妥协/更可持续的经济/被斥天真）均无关，亦非动词，对(3)(4)(9)(10)及所有空均不可填；audience(观众)虽与 hearing 同有“受众”义，但固定搭配为 receive a hearing（得到审议机会），receive an audience 指“接见”语义不合(8)，且 audience 不填其他空；reluctantly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Few things shape our sense of who we are as profoundly as the language we speak. Long before we can articulate a single opinion, the tongue of our community quietly (1)____ the very categories through which we perceive the world. To lose that language, as countless minority communities have discovered, is to feel a part of one's identity steadily (2)____ away. This is why campaigns to revive endangered tongues are rarely about communication alone; they are, at heart, struggles to preserve a distinctive way of (3)____ reality. Yet the relationship between language and identity is seldom simple. Migrants, for instance, often find themselves (4)____ between two linguistic worlds, fully at home in neither. Children of such families may grow fluent in the dominant language while their grandparents' speech becomes a (5)____ inheritance, half remembered and rarely spoken. Some celebrate this fluidity as a (6)____ form of belonging, broader and more generous than any single nation could offer. Others mourn what is irretrievably (7)____ when a mother tongue falls silent. Linguists, meanwhile, caution against treating any language as a fixed (8)____ ; every living tongue is constantly borrowing, shedding and reinventing itself. What remains beyond serious (9)____ is that the words available to us subtly (10)____ the thoughts we are able to think. To defend a language, then, is to defend a particular and irreplaceable lens upon human experience. Each tongue encodes centuries of observation, the proverbs and turns of phrase through which a people has made sense of its existence. When the last fluent speaker dies, something no museum can store is extinguished forever. This is why the patient work of teachers, grandparents and recording linguists matters so profoundly, even when the numbers are heartbreakingly small.
sets.explain_zh: (1) shapes 塑造，三单及物动词（主语 the tongue，宾语 categories）；(2) slipping 流逝，feel...steadily slipping away 现在分词（slip away 溜走，与 feel sth doing 结构呼应）；(3) interpreting 解读，a way of interpreting reality 介词 of 后接动名词（解读现实的方式）；(4) suspended 悬置，find oneself suspended between two worlds 过去分词（夹在两者之间）；(5) fragmentary 零碎的，a fragmentary inheritance 形容词（与 half remembered 呼应）；(6) richer 更丰富的，a richer form of belonging 形容词比较级修饰 form；(7) lost 失去的，what is irretrievably lost 过去分词（被永久失去之物）；(8) monument 纪念碑/固定不变之物，treating language as a fixed monument 名词（把语言当作凝固的纪念物）；(9) question 疑问，beyond question 固定搭配名词（毫无疑问）；(10) constrain 制约，words constrain the thoughts 及物动词原形（词汇限制可想之事）。干扰项：plywood(胶合板)为材质名词，既非动词（(1)(10)需三单/原形及物动词均不可填），作名词亦无法填入(8)“a fixed ___”或(9)“beyond ___”（a fixed plywood / beyond plywood 不通），更不能修饰(5)(6)的 inheritance/form，对所有空均不可填；draining(排干)与(2)slip away 的“悄然流逝”相比，drain away 多指液体/精力，feel identity slipping away 才地道，draining 语义偏“耗尽”不合(2)；aluminium(铝)为材质名词，非动词不可填(3)等动词空，作名词亦无法填(8)(9)，更不能修饰(5)(6)，与 interpreting/describing 所在的“解读现实方式”毫无关联，对所有空均不可填；wealthier(更富裕的)指物质财富，与(6)form of belonging 的“内涵更丰富”应为 richer，wealthier 语义不合；constantly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: The so-called gig economy was sold to the public as a triumph of flexibility, a world in which anyone could earn a living on their own terms. The reality experienced by millions of platform workers has proved considerably more (1)____ . Drivers and couriers enjoy the freedom to choose their hours, but that freedom is (2)____ at the cost of the security their parents took for granted. Classified as independent contractors rather than employees, they are routinely (3)____ of sick pay, pensions and the right to bargain collectively. When an algorithm silently (4)____ their access to work, there is often no human being to appeal to and no explanation (5)____ . Defenders insist that such arrangements suit those who value autonomy above all, and for some that is genuinely (6)____ . But the broader social cost is rarely entered into the ledger. As more jobs are stripped of their traditional (7)____ , the burden of illness, accident and old age quietly shifts from companies onto individuals and, ultimately, onto the state. Communities, too, suffer when work becomes so (8)____ that no one stays long enough to form lasting ties. Reformers therefore (9)____ that platforms be required to extend basic protections, arguing that flexibility and fairness need not be mutually exclusive. Until that happens, the convenience enjoyed by consumers will continue to rest, however discreetly, upon the (10)____ of those who deliver it. Flexibility was never inherently bad; many workers genuinely prefer to set their own schedules. The problem lies not in flexibility itself but in the bargain silently attached to it, whereby autonomy is granted only if every traditional cushion against misfortune is removed. A fairer arrangement would let people keep the freedom they prize without forfeiting their basic dignity.
sets.explain_zh: (1) sobering 发人深省/严峻的，proved more sobering 表语形容词（现实远比宣传严峻）；(2) purchased 换取/以代价获得，that freedom is purchased at the cost of... 过去分词被动（这种自由是以牺牲安全为代价换来的，be purchased at the cost of 固定搭配）；(3) deprived 被剥夺，be deprived of 固定搭配（后有 of，被剥夺病假等）；(4) suspends 暂停/中止，algorithm silently suspends their access 三单及物动词（暂停其接单权限）；(5) forthcoming 提供出来的，no explanation forthcoming 表语形容词（无解释可得，固定用法 be forthcoming）；(6) true 真实成立的，for some that is genuinely true 表语形容词（对某些人确实如此）；(7) safeguards 保障措施，stripped of their traditional safeguards 名词（被剥去传统保障）；(8) precarious 不稳定的，work becomes so precarious 表语形容词（工作如此朝不保夕）；(9) demand 要求，demand that platforms be required 及物动词原形后接虚拟语气从句；(10) vulnerability 脆弱处境，rest upon the vulnerability of those 名词（建立在劳动者的脆弱之上）。干扰项：lunar(月球的)为属性形容词，既非动词（(2)(3)(4)(9)需被动分词/三单/原形动词均不可填），也无法作表语修饰(1)(5)(6)(8)的语境（proved more lunar / explanation lunar / genuinely lunar / so lunar 均不通），对所有空均不可填；tidal(潮汐的)同为属性形容词，非动词，亦不能填(1)(5)(6)(8)任何表语空，对所有空均不可填；pebbles(鹅卵石)为复数名词，填(7)“stripped of their traditional pebbles”或(10)“rest upon the pebbles of those”均语义荒谬，亦非动词不可填动词空，对所有空均不可填；encouraging(鼓舞的)与(1)句“现实更严峻 sobering”语义相反；willingly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: For most of human history, the climate could be treated as a fixed backdrop against which the human drama unfolded. That assumption no longer holds. As the planet warms, communities everywhere are being compelled to (1)____ to conditions their ancestors never faced. Adaptation, once a marginal concern, has moved to the very (2)____ of climate policy. Coastal cities are raising sea walls and rethinking where it is even (3)____ to build; farmers are switching to drought-resistant crops and revising the calendars that governed planting for generations. Such measures are expensive, and the resources available are distributed with painful (4)____ : the regions least responsible for warming are frequently those least able to (5)____ themselves against its consequences. This injustice has become a (6)____ point in international negotiations, where poorer nations demand support that wealthier ones are reluctant to (7)____ . Yet adaptation, however necessary, is no substitute for cutting emissions; it merely buys (8)____ time. Some scientists warn against a dangerous (9)____ , whereby the apparent success of sea walls and air conditioning lulls societies into believing the underlying problem has been solved. The hard truth is that adaptation and mitigation must advance together, for neither alone will (10)____ . A civilisation that learns to live with a changing climate while still refusing to stabilise it would be merely postponing, not escaping, the reckoning. The seductive appeal of adaptation is that its benefits are immediate and visible, whereas the rewards of cutting emissions are diffuse and delayed. Wisdom, however, lies in treating the two strategies as the inseparable halves of a single response, for a sea wall built today protects nobody if the seas it holds back simply keep on rising.
sets.explain_zh: (1) adjust 调整适应，adjust to conditions 不及物动词（后有 to，适应新条件）；(2) centre 中心，move to the very centre of policy 名词（成为政策核心）；(3) feasible 行得通的，rethinking where it is even feasible to build 表语形容词（重新思考何处还能建造，搭配 it is feasible to do）；(4) inequality 不平等，distributed with painful inequality 名词（资源分配极不公）；(5) defend 保护防御，defend themselves against 及物动词（后有 against，抵御后果）；(6) sticking 卡住的，a sticking point 固定搭配（争议焦点/僵持点），形容词性现在分词作定语；(7) provide 提供，support that...reluctant to provide 及物动词原形（不愿提供支持）；(8) valuable 宝贵的，buys valuable time 形容词修饰 time（争取宝贵时间）；(9) complacency 自满，a dangerous complacency 名词（危险的自满）；(10) suffice 足够，neither alone will suffice 不及物动词（单靠任一都不够）。干扰项：horizontal(水平的)为方位形容词，既非动词（(1)(5)(7)(10)需原形动词均不可填），也无法填(3)(6)(8)(9)的表语/定语/名词空（it is even horizontal to build / a horizontal point / buys horizontal time / a dangerous horizontal 均不通），对所有空均不可填；edge(边缘)与(2)centre 语义相反（应为“移至中心”而非边缘），且 edge 不填其他空；scenic(风景优美的)为形容词但语义与(3)“是否还能/适宜建造”完全无关（建造的可行性而非景色），且不接 to do，对所有空均不可填；gravel(砾石)为不可数名词，填(4)“distributed with painful gravel”或(2)(9)的“政策中心/危险的自满”均语义荒谬，亦非动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填；grudgingly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Leisure is one of those concepts that everyone claims to value yet few pause to examine. We speak of it as mere rest, the empty interval between bouts of productive work, and in doing so we badly (1)____ its true significance. The ancient philosophers held a strikingly different view: for them, leisure was not the absence of activity but its highest (2)____ , the free space in which a human being could think, create and simply become fully (3)____ . Modern life has all but (4)____ that ideal. Our so-called free time is increasingly colonised by the same restless logic that governs the workplace, as we anxiously (5)____ our hobbies into achievements and our holidays into content to be shared. Even relaxation has become a task to be (6)____ efficiently. The result is a curious paradox: people work longer in order to afford leisure they no longer know how to (7)____ . Reclaiming the older meaning would require a quiet act of (8)____ , a willingness to do something for no reason beyond the doing of it. That, however, runs directly against a culture that measures every hour by its (9)____ . Perhaps the most radical thing a person can do today is to spend an afternoon (10)____ , neither producing nor consuming, but merely present. In such unhurried moments, some would argue, we recover a freedom that ceaseless busyness has quietly stolen from us. There is a deep irony in saving time only to fill every recovered minute with fresh obligations. The older tradition saw it differently, treating contemplative leisure as the soil in which philosophy and friendship take root. Recovering a fragment of that wisdom might do more for our wellbeing than the efficiency we anxiously pursue instead.
sets.explain_zh: (1) misjudge 误判，badly misjudge its significance 及物动词原形（严重误判其意义）；(2) purpose 目的，its highest purpose 名词（最高目的，与 not the absence of activity 对照）；(3) alive 鲜活的，become fully alive 表语形容词（充分鲜活地存在，与 think/create 呼应）；(4) abandoned 抛弃，has all but abandoned that ideal 及物动词过去分词（现在完成，几乎抛弃了理想）；(5) convert 转化，convert A into B 固定搭配及物动词原形（后有 into，把爱好变成成就）；(6) performed 执行完成，a task to be performed efficiently 过去分词（被高效完成的任务）；(7) enjoy 享受，no longer know how to enjoy 及物动词原形（不知如何享受闲暇）；(8) rebellion 反叛，a quiet act of rebellion 名词（无声的反叛之举）；(9) output 产出，measures every hour by its output 名词（以产出衡量每小时）；(10) idle 闲着的，spend an afternoon idle 表语形容词（无所事事地度过下午，与 neither producing nor consuming 呼应）。干扰项：marble(大理石)为材质名词，既非动词（(1)(5)(7)需原形动词均不可填），作名词填(2)(8)(9)（its highest marble / an act of marble / by its marble）或作表语(3)(10)均语义荒谬，对所有空均不可填；meaning(意义)虽近 purpose，但(2)句 its highest ___ 强调“最高目的/功用”，且行文末段已用 meaning（the older meaning）易重复，purpose 与 not the absence of activity 的“为了什么”对照更准，meaning 作干扰；awake(醒着的)与(3)become fully ___ 应为“充分鲜活 alive”（醒着仅指清醒，与 think/create/become 的存在状态不符）；bronze(青铜)为材质名词，非动词不可填(4)(6)等动词分词空（has all but bronze / to be bronze 不通），作名词/表语填(2)(8)(9)(3)(10)均语义荒谬，对所有空均不可填；consume(消耗/消费)与(7)enjoy 语境不合（不知如何“享受”而非“消费”闲暇），且(10)句 neither producing nor consuming 已用 consume，语义重复且词性为动词原形不作表语，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Every era likes to imagine that its inventions are uniquely disruptive, but the tension between innovation and regulation is as old as technology itself. New tools almost always arrive faster than the rules meant to govern them, leaving lawmakers (1)____ to catch up. The temptation, on one side, is to regulate aggressively, smothering a promising technology before its dangers are even properly (2)____ . On the other side lies the opposite error: allowing powerful innovations to spread unchecked until the harm becomes all but (3)____ to reverse. Striking the balance is difficult, partly because the costs and benefits of any new technology are rarely (4)____ at the outset. Self-driving cars, gene editing and artificial intelligence each promise enormous gains while posing risks that no one can confidently (5)____ in advance. Regulators who move too slowly may permit real damage; those who move too quickly may strangle the very (6)____ that would have made everyone better off. A wiser approach treats regulation not as a brake but as a (7)____ , steering invention toward useful ends rather than merely blocking it. This demands institutions agile enough to (8)____ their rules as understanding deepens, and humble enough to admit when they have erred. It also requires that innovators accept some (9)____ for the consequences of what they unleash. Technology, after all, is never neutral; the real question is whether we will (10)____ its direction deliberately, or simply be carried along wherever it happens to take us. History is littered with both kinds of failure: the heavy hand that crushed a useful advance, and the permissiveness that let a harmful one entrench itself. Neither caution nor enthusiasm is a virtue alone; what matters is knowing which a moment demands.
sets.explain_zh: (1) scrambling 手忙脚乱地努力，leaving lawmakers scrambling to catch up 现在分词（立法者疲于追赶，scramble to do 手忙脚乱地做）；(2) understood 被理解，before its dangers are properly understood 被动过去分词（危险尚未被充分认识）；(3) impossible 不可能的，becomes all but impossible to reverse 表语形容词（几乎无法逆转）；(4) evident 显而易见的，costs and benefits are rarely evident at the outset 表语形容词（一开始难以显现）；(5) quantify 量化估算，no one can confidently quantify in advance 及物动词原形（无人能预先量化风险）；(6) progress 进步成果，strangle the very progress 名词（扼杀本可惠及众人的进步）；(7) rudder 舵，not as a brake but as a rudder 名词（不是刹车而是方向舵，与 steering 呼应）；(8) revise 修订，agile enough to revise their rules 及物动词原形（随认识加深修订规则）；(9) responsibility 责任，accept some responsibility for 名词（为后果承担责任）；(10) steer 掌舵引导，steer its direction deliberately 及物动词原形（主动把握技术方向）。干扰项：circular(圆形的/循环的)为形容词，既非动词（(1)需现在分词、(5)(8)(10)需原形动词均不可填），也无法填(3)(4)的表语（all but circular to reverse / rarely circular at the outset 均不通）或(6)(7)(9)名词空，对所有空均不可填；metallic(金属的)为材质形容词，非动词不可填(2)(5)(8)(10)，作表语填(3)(4)或名词空(6)(7)(9)均语义荒谬，对所有空均不可填；difficult(困难的)与(3)句 all but ___ to reverse 应为“几乎不可能 impossible”（difficult 程度不足且 all but difficult 不地道），语义/搭配失败；sawdust(木屑)为不可数名词，填(6)“strangle the very sawdust”、(7)“as a sawdust”或(9)“accept some sawdust”均语义荒谬，亦非动词/形容词，对所有空均不可填；blindly(副词)无对应副词空，对所有空均不可填。

sets.passage: Trust in public institutions has fallen to levels that would once have seemed unimaginable. Across many democracies, citizens express deep (1)____ toward governments, courts, banks and the press alike, suspecting each of serving private interests rather than the common good. The causes of this collapse are tangled. Repeated scandals have understandably (2)____ confidence, and every fresh revelation of corruption seems to (3)____ the cynical assumption that the powerful always look after themselves. Economic shocks have played their part too, for institutions that fail to (4)____ ordinary people from hardship are quickly judged to have broken an implicit promise. Social media, meanwhile, amplifies every grievance while making patient, (5)____ explanation almost impossible to hear. The consequences are grave. A society in which no one trusts the referee cannot easily resolve its disputes, and the resulting vacuum is readily filled by demagogues who promise to (6)____ a corrupt establishment. Yet the picture is not wholly bleak. Trust, researchers note, is not destroyed at random; it is forfeited when institutions behave (7)____ and earned back when they behave well. Rebuilding it therefore demands not clever public relations but a sustained record of (8)____ conduct, transparency and visible fairness. This is slow, unglamorous work, easily (9)____ by a single fresh betrayal. Still, the alternative, a populace permanently convinced that every authority is rotten, is a recipe for paralysis. Restoring faith in institutions may be the quiet (10)____ on which much else now depends. The temptation, for those in authority, is to treat declining trust as a communications problem curable by smarter messaging. That diagnosis is dangerous, for it encourages the very evasions that corroded confidence in the first place. People are not fooled for long by words unmatched by deeds.
sets.explain_zh: (1) distrust 不信任，express deep distrust toward 名词（对…表达深深的不信任）；(2) shaken 动摇，have understandably shaken confidence 及物动词过去分词（现在完成，动摇了信心）；(3) reinforce 强化，reinforce the cynical assumption 及物动词原形（每次曝光强化愤世假设）；(4) shield 保护使免于，shield ordinary people from hardship 及物动词（后有 from，使民众免受困苦，固定搭配 shield sb from sth）；(5) nuanced 细致入微的，patient, nuanced explanation 形容词修饰 explanation（耐心而细致的解释）；(6) overthrow 推翻，promise to overthrow a corrupt establishment 及物动词原形（承诺推翻腐败建制）；(7) badly 恶劣地，institutions behave badly 副词修饰 behave（与后文 behave well 对照）；(8) honest 诚实的，a record of honest conduct 形容词修饰 conduct（诚实作为的记录）；(9) undone 被毁掉，easily undone by a single betrayal 过去分词（轻易被一次背叛毁掉，与 rebuilding 对照）；(10) precondition 前提条件，the quiet precondition on which much depends 名词（许多事所依赖的前提）。干扰项：limestone(石灰岩)为材质名词，填(1)“express deep limestone toward”或(10)“the quiet limestone on which”均语义荒谬，亦非动词/形容词/副词，对所有空均不可填；broken(破碎/打破)虽与 shaken 同含“受损”，但 scandals have ___ confidence 地道为 shaken confidence，break confidence 不成立，且(9)需 undone（前功尽弃）而非 broken，broken 无最佳落点作干扰；granite(花岗岩)为材质名词，非动词不可填(3)(4)(6)的动词空（seems to granite / fail to granite / promise to granite 均不通），作名词填(1)(10)亦荒谬，对所有空均不可填；guilty(有罪的/内疚的)为形容词，(4)需及物动词 + from（shield sb from hardship），guilty 既非动词也不接此结构，且 a record of guilty conduct 语义不合(8)所需的“诚实”褒义，对所有空均不可填；carelessly(副词)与(7)badly 同为副词修饰 behave，但(7)与 behave well 对照需 badly（恶劣 vs 良好），carelessly（粗心地）不构成该对照，故失败作干扰。
